title: eriogonum brevicaule subsp. orendense
intro: Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. orendense is a subspecies of the Eriogonum brevicaule plant, commonly known as short-flowered buckwheat or Orendorff's buckwheat, which is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. orendense include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
